questions.question: Define a cyclic group and give two examples of cyclic groups with their generators.
keyPoints: Definition: A cyclic group is generated by a single element
Every element is some power of the generator
Example 1: (Z,+) with generator 1
Example 2: Zn under addition modulo n with generator [1]

questions.question: State and prove the Fundamental Theorem of Cyclic Groups regarding subgroups.
keyPoints: Statement: Every subgroup of a cyclic group is cyclic
For each divisor d of n, there exists exactly one subgroup of order d
Proof using generator properties and divisibility
Show subgroup generated by a^k where k is smallest positive integer in subgroup

questions.question: Find all generators of Z12. Explain how you determined them.
keyPoints: Generators are elements relatively prime to 12
φ(12) = 4 generators
Verification that each generates all 12 elements

questions.question: State Fermat's Little Theorem and Euler's Theorem. How are they related to cyclic groups?
keyPoints: Fermat: a^(p-1) ≡ 1 (mod p) for prime p, gcd(a,p)=1
Euler: a^φ(n) ≡ 1 (mod n) for gcd(a,n)=1
Both arise from cyclic nature of (Z/nZ)*
The multiplicative group of units forms a cyclic structure

questions.question: If G is a cyclic group of order 20, list all subgroups and their orders. Justify your answer.
keyPoints: By Fundamental Theorem, one subgroup for each divisor
Subgroup orders: 1, 2, 4, 5, 10, 20
Exactly one subgroup for each divisor

questions.question: Determine the order of each element in Z8: [0], [1], [2], [3], [4], [5], [6], [7]
keyPoints: [0]: order 1 (identity)
[1]: order 8 (generator)
[2]: order 4
[3]: order 8
[4]: order 2
[5]: order 8
[6]: order 4
[7]: order 8

questions.question: Prove that every cyclic group is abelian.
keyPoints: Let G be cyclic generated by g
Take any two elements a = g^m, b = g^n
Compute ab = g^m . g^n = g^(m+n)
Compute ba = g^n . g^m = g^(n+m) = g^(m+n) = ab
Therefore commutative/abelian
